semanticCore: Hunger, starvation, famine, fatigue from hunger, or empty stomach

keyDerivations.meaning: Severe hunger, famine, starvation, or empty-bellied distress

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَوْ إِطْعَامٌ فِي يَوْمٍ ذِي مَسْغَبَةٍ (90:14): Or feeding on a day of severe hunger/famine.
summaryEn: The root represents famine or empty stomach hunger (Saghb), encouraging charity and feeding the poor, especially during periods of widespread food shortage and economic hardship.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الجوع الشديد المقترن بالتعب والجهد وحالة المجاعة العامة (سغب)، مرغباً في إطعام المساكين واليتامى في أوقات الشدة والمسغبة.
rawText: اسغب 4 He (a man, TA) entered upon a state of hunger. (K.) سَاغِبٌ and 🡻سَغْبَانُ (S, A, Msb, K) and 🡻سَغِبٌ (K) Hungry: (S, A, Msb, K, TA:) or suffering hunger together with fatigue: (A, Msb, K:) or thirsty: (TA:) fem. [of the second] 🡻سَغْبَى, (S, K,) pl. سِغَابٌ. (K.) You say also, هُوَ سَاغِبٌ لَاغِبٌ (A, TA) and 🡻سَغْبَانُلَغْبَانُ (TA) [He is hungry and fatigued, or fatigued in the utmost degree]. And it is said in a trad., 🡻مُسْغِبُونَدَخَلَ خَيْبَرَ وَهُمْ, expl. as meaning [He entered Kheyber] they being hungry. (TA.) سَغَبٌ an inf. n. of سَغِبَ [q. v.]. (S, &c.) === It also signifies Thirst: but is unused: (K:) [or] thirst is sometimes thus termed. (Msb.) سَغِبٌ : see سَاغِبٌ. سَغْبَانُ ; and its fem., سَغْبَى: see the next paragraph, in three places. سَغْبَةٌ Hunger: or hunger combined with fatigue. (TA.) [See also 1.] مُسْغِبٌ : see its pl. in what next precedes. هُوَ مُسَغَّبٌ لَهُ كَذَا and مَسَعَّبٌ [the latter written in the CK مُسْغَبٌ] To him is allowed, or permitted, such a thing. (K, TA.)
